MANCHESTER HEALTHCARE CENTER

CCN: 555273 · LOS ANGELES, CA 90044 · Los Angeles County

Address
837 W. MANCHESTER AVE., LOS ANGELES, CA 90044

3237531789

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.18 4.08 3.96 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.34 0.71 0.32 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.37 0.90 1.29 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.47 2.46 2.34 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.71 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.79 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.70
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.19
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.50
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.44
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.05
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-02 35 21 14 240 1 240
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-02-04 24 10 14 343 1 343
Total weighted health score
265.8
lower is better

Deficiencies (77)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
74 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-12-16 2026-01-06
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-12-12 2026-01-06
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-12-12 2026-01-06
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-12-12 2026-01-06
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2025-12-12 2026-01-06
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2025-07-07 2025-07-18
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-07-07 2025-07-18
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-07-07 2025-07-18
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. G 2025-06-18 2025-07-09
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-06-18 2025-07-09
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-04-02 2025-04-25

0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-04-02 2025-04-25
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. E 2025-04-02 2025-04-25
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2025-02-26 2025-03-13
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. E 2025-02-26 2025-03-13
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-02-26 2025-03-13
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. F 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0645 PASARR screening for Mental disorders or Intellectual Disabilities D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. F 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0732 Post nurse staffing information every day. F 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. F 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0770 Provide timely, quality laboratory services/tests to meet the needs of residents. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. F 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2025-02-02 2025-02-21
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. E 2025-01-21 2025-02-10
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-04-26 2024-05-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-04-26 2024-05-26
0727 Have a registered nurse on duty 8 hours a day; and select a registered nurse to be the director of nurses on a full time basis. F 2024-04-26 2024-05-26
